hook.headline: Three.js + R3F just made GLSL feel old.

cards.label: 01 · tsl
cards.title: Three Shading Language is what GLSL should've been.
cards.body: TSL lets you write shaders in JS — same expressiveness as GLSL, but typed, composable, and transpiled to both WebGL and WebGPU automatically. No more separate .glsl files.

cards.label: 02 · r3f
cards.title: R3F has first-class TSL hooks.
cards.body: React Three Fiber added TSL support natively — useTslShader, MeshTslMaterial, the whole pipeline. Same React-component-mental-model you already use, just with shader nodes.

cards.label: 03 · post
cards.title: postprocessing v7 = effect chains made composable.
cards.body: Bloom + chromatic aberration + film grain + custom shader, in a single declarative React tree. Effects are now functions — pipe them together, animate any prop, hot-reload everything.

cards.label: 04 · webgpu
cards.title: WebGPU is finally everywhere.
cards.body: Safari shipped WebGPU in 2025. Chrome and Edge have had it for years. Three.js + R3F let you write once → run on the new GPU backend automatically. ~2× faster on compute-heavy scenes.

cards.label: 05 · start
cards.title: Start here.
cards.body: 1) `npx create-r3f@latest`. 2) Read drei docs (200+ helpers). 3) Steal from poimandres.github.io. 4) When you're ready, swap MeshStandardMaterial → MeshTslMaterial and start writing nodes.

take.use_if: You're building anything with 3D on the web — TSL + postprocessing is the modern stack worth learning.
take.skip_if: You're shipping a content site with one hero animation — Lottie or Rive is lighter and easier.
